rawText:
When Rachel Reeves, Britain's chancellor, declared in her Mais lecture that the era of a globetrotting post-Brexit Britain capable of doing without Europe was over, she was not merely torching a decade of orthodoxies about her country's place in the world -- she was articulating a broader continental anxiety about economic identity in an age of rising protectionism, geopolitical fracture, and the uncomfortable realisation that geography, far from being obsolete, has reasserted itself with a vengeance.

The post-Brexit fantasy that a "nimble" Britain could leap between the rival blocs of Europe, America, and China has collided with the reality that trade deals beyond Europe make less impact on growth than a single agricultural agreement with the EU, that Britain's biggest trading partner remains stubbornly continental despite all the barriers erected since 2016, and that Britons themselves would now vote to rejoin the bloc by a margin of fifty-two per cent to twenty-nine per cent.

Across the Channel, Germany's own crisis of economic identity has taken a different but equally revealing form, as a country once known as Europe's austerity champion embarks on an extraordinary fiscal splurge -- a five-hundred-billion-euro debt-financed infrastructure fund and a defence ramp-up from forty-seven billion euros in 2021 to a projected one hundred and eight billion this year -- in the hope that rearmament can jolt a stagnant industrial machine back to life.

The logic of Germany's gamble is that state-driven demand for armoured vehicles, air-defence systems, and AI-enabled drone technology will generate downstream effects across the whole economy, that some of the fourteen thousand manufacturing workers shed each month might be redeployed to defence production, and that the lattice of startups clustered around Munich working on robotics and autonomous systems could eventually close the gap between military and civilian innovation.

Yet for all its ambition, German recovery remains tantalisingly out of reach -- manufacturing output has fallen fifteen per cent from its 2018 peak, bottlenecks in facilities and labour supply have created huge order backlogs, and growth this year may prove even slower than the government's already-downgraded forecast of one per cent, prompting one bank analyst to compare the economy to a ketchup bottle that refuses to pour until, suddenly, everything comes out at once.

Britain's economic data, meanwhile, present their own epistemological puzzle, since the Office for National Statistics relies on three imperfect data sources that it triangulates into an estimate so provisional that quarterly GDP growth typically does not stabilise for a decade, with revisions since 2012 increasing by roughly half owing to declining survey response rates -- a reminder that the numbers on which political narratives are built are shifting sands rather than solid ground.

The Iran crisis, which has sent gas prices spiking across the continent, has added a brutal layer of urgency to Europe's economic rethinking, presenting Britain's Treasury with the prospect of yet another expensive household bail-out while simultaneously making the case for energy-market integration with the EU -- an argument that even the most committed Brexiteers find difficult to rebut when petrol prices leap eight pence in a fortnight.

Paradoxically, motoring in Britain has rarely been cheaper in real terms, because fuel taxes are so high that pump prices are less responsive to global oil-market gyrations, because fleet-wide fuel efficiency has improved by two-thirds over three decades, and because fourteen years of frozen fuel duty -- costing taxpayers a hundred and twenty billion pounds -- have insulated drivers from the true cost of their consumption.

What unites these disparate threads -- Britain's tilt back toward Brussels, Germany's attempt to rearm its way to growth, the unreliability of GDP statistics, and the hidden cheapness of British motoring -- is a continent struggling to reconcile inherited economic models with a world in which America is turning inward, China is dumping surplus production, and the old certainties of free trade and transatlantic alliance have been replaced by a scramble for strategic autonomy.

Europe's emerging economic identity, if it has one, is defined not by the bold confidence of a superpower but by the pragmatic recognition that alignment with neighbours is preferable to isolation, that fiscal orthodoxy must sometimes yield to strategic necessity, and that the most honest measure of economic health is not the headline GDP figure but whether the institutions and infrastructure exist to deliver prosperity that citizens can actually feel -- a test that neither Britain nor Germany, for all their statistical revisions and fiscal ambitions, has yet convincingly passed.

overview.title: Europe's Search for a New Economic Identity

sentences.original: When Rachel Reeves, Britain's chancellor, declared in her Mais lecture that the era of a globetrotting post-Brexit Britain capable of doing without Europe was over, she was not merely torching a decade of orthodoxies about her country's place in the world -- she was articulating a broader continental anxiety about economic identity in an age of rising protectionism, geopolitical fracture, and the uncomfortable realisation that geography, far from being obsolete, has reasserted itself with a vengeance.
sentences.translation: 레이철 리브스 영국 재무장관이 마이스 강연에서 유럽 없이도 해낼 수 있는 세계를 누비는 브렉시트 이후 영국의 시대는 끝났다고 선언했을 때, 그녀는 단지 10년간의 정통 견해를 불태운 것만이 아니었다 -- 보호무역주의 확산, 지정학적 균열, 그리고 지리적 요인이 결코 구식이 되기는커녕 맹렬하게 다시 힘을 발휘하고 있다는 불편한 자각의 시대에 경제적 정체성에 대한 보다 광범위한 대륙적 불안을 표현한 것이었다.

grammarElements.text: When Rachel Reeves declared ... that the era ... was over
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 + that 명사절

grammarElements.text: she was not merely torching ... -- she was articulating ...
grammarElements.label: not merely A -- B 대조 구문

grammarElements.text: a broader continental anxiety about economic identity
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: geography, far from being obsolete, has reasserted itself with a vengeance
grammarElements.label: far from + 동명사 삽입절

vocabulary.example: The new policy challenged decades of economic orthodoxy.
Geopolitical tensions have disrupted trade routes across the region.

expressions.usage: 한동안 잠잠하던 것이 강력하게 되돌아왔을 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Subject] was not merely [action A] -- [subject] was [action B]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 행동 너머의 더 깊은 의미를 밝히는 패턴
examples: The CEO was not merely resigning -- she was signalling a total break with the board.
The ruling was not merely a legal verdict -- it was rewriting the boundary between state and market.

drillQuestions.question: She was not merely torching a decade of ______ about her country's place in the world.

sentences.notes: not merely ... she was articulating 구문이 한 정치인의 발언을 유럽 전체의 정체성 위기로 확장시키는 수사적 장치이다.

koreanAlignment.en: torching a decade of orthodoxies
a broader continental anxiety about economic identity
koreanAlignment.ko: 경제적 정체성에 대한 보다 광범위한 대륙적 불안

koreanAlignment.en: geography, far from being obsolete
has reasserted itself with a vengeance

sentences.original: The post-Brexit fantasy that a "nimble" Britain could leap between the rival blocs of Europe, America, and China has collided with the reality that trade deals beyond Europe make less impact on growth than a single agricultural agreement with the EU, that Britain's biggest trading partner remains stubbornly continental despite all the barriers erected since 2016, and that Britons themselves would now vote to rejoin the bloc by a margin of fifty-two per cent to twenty-nine per cent.
sentences.translation: "민첩한" 영국이 유럽, 미국, 중국이라는 경쟁 블록 사이를 뛰어다닐 수 있다는 브렉시트 이후의 환상은, EU 외부와의 무역 협정이 EU와의 농업 협정 하나보다 성장에 미치는 영향이 적다는 현실, 2016년 이후 세워진 모든 장벽에도 불구하고 영국의 최대 교역 상대가 완고하게 유럽 대륙에 남아 있다는 현실, 그리고 영국인 스스로 52퍼센트 대 29퍼센트의 차이로 블록 재가입에 투표할 것이라는 현실과 충돌했다.

grammarElements.text: The post-Brexit fantasy that a 'nimble' Britain could leap ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 that절이 붙은 주어

grammarElements.text: has collided with
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: the reality that ... , that ... , and that ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 that절 목적어

vocabulary.example: Smaller firms are often more nimble than their larger rivals.

expressions.usage: 기대나 환상이 실제 상황과 정면으로 부딪혔을 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The [fantasy/assumption] that [claim] has collided with the reality that [fact 1], that [fact 2], and that [fact 3]
patterns.explanation: 환상과 현실을 대비시키면서 현실의 근거를 삼중으로 나열하는 패턴
examples: The assumption that remote work would fade has collided with the reality that productivity stayed stable, that employees resisted returning, and that office leases were not renewed.
The belief that inflation was temporary has collided with the reality that prices kept rising, that wages lagged, and that central banks had to act aggressively.

drillQuestions.question: The post-Brexit fantasy has ______ with the reality that trade deals beyond Europe make less impact on growth.

sentences.notes: fantasy와 reality의 충돌이라는 프레임이 브렉시트 비전의 붕괴를 압축적으로 보여주며, 삼중 that절이 증거를 체계적으로 쌓는다.

koreanAlignment.en: The post-Brexit fantasy
a 'nimble' Britain could leap between the rival blocs
koreanAlignment.ko: 민첩한 영국이 경쟁 블록 사이를 뛰어다닐 수 있다는

koreanAlignment.en: has collided with the reality
by a margin of fifty-two per cent to twenty-nine per cent

sentences.original: Across the Channel, Germany's own crisis of economic identity has taken a different but equally revealing form, as a country once known as Europe's austerity champion embarks on an extraordinary fiscal splurge -- a five-hundred-billion-euro debt-financed infrastructure fund and a defence ramp-up from forty-seven billion euros in 2021 to a projected one hundred and eight billion this year -- in the hope that rearmament can jolt a stagnant industrial machine back to life.
sentences.translation: 해협 건너편에서, 독일 고유의 경제적 정체성 위기는 다르지만 마찬가지로 시사적인 형태를 띠고 있다. 한때 유럽의 긴축 챔피언으로 알려진 나라가 전례 없는 재정 지출에 나서고 있기 때문이다 -- 5000억 유로 규모의 부채 조달 인프라 기금과 2021년 470억 유로에서 올해 예상 1080억 유로로의 국방 증액 -- 재무장이 정체된 산업 기계에 활력을 되돌려줄 수 있다는 희망 속에서.

grammarElements.text: Across the Channel
grammarElements.label: 장소 부사구

grammarElements.text: Germany's own crisis of economic identity
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has taken a different but equally revealing form
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: as a country once known as Europe's austerity champion embarks on ...
grammarElements.label: 이유/동시 as절

grammarElements.text: in the hope that rearmament can jolt ... back to life
grammarElements.label: 목적/희망의 전치사구

vocabulary.example: Years of austerity left public services in a weakened state.

expressions.usage: 정체된 경제나 시스템을 되살릴 때 사용하는 역동적 표현

patterns.pattern: Across [location], [subject]'s crisis has taken [form], as [entity] embarks on [action] in the hope that [outcome]
patterns.explanation: 장소를 전환하면서 다른 주체의 유사한 위기를 소개하고 대응 방향을 제시하는 패턴
examples: Across the Pacific, Japan's demographic crisis has taken a different form, as a country once known for lifetime employment embarks on immigration reform in the hope that newcomers can fill the labour gap.
Across the border, Canada's housing crisis has taken an acute form, as a nation once famed for affordable cities embarks on massive construction in the hope that supply can catch demand.

drillQuestions.question: Germany embarks on an extraordinary fiscal ______ in the hope that rearmament can jolt a stagnant industrial machine back to life.

sentences.notes: austerity champion에서 fiscal splurge로의 전환이 독일 경제 정체성의 극적인 변화를 한 문장으로 담는다.

koreanAlignment.en: Across the Channel
Europe's austerity champion
an extraordinary fiscal splurge
jolt a stagnant industrial machine back to life

sentences.original: The logic of Germany's gamble is that state-driven demand for armoured vehicles, air-defence systems, and AI-enabled drone technology will generate downstream effects across the whole economy, that some of the fourteen thousand manufacturing workers shed each month might be redeployed to defence production, and that the lattice of startups clustered around Munich working on robotics and autonomous systems could eventually close the gap between military and civilian innovation.
sentences.translation: 독일의 도박이 따르는 논리는, 장갑차, 방공 시스템, AI 기반 드론 기술에 대한 국가 주도 수요가 경제 전반에 걸쳐 파급 효과를 일으킬 것이라는 점, 매달 감원되는 1만 4000명의 제조업 노동자 중 일부가 국방 생산으로 재배치될 수 있다는 점, 그리고 뮌헨 주변에 모여 로봇공학과 자율 시스템을 연구하는 스타트업 네트워크가 궁극적으로 군사와 민간 혁신 사이의 격차를 좁힐 수 있다는 점이다.

grammarElements.text: The logic of Germany's gamble
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: be동사

grammarElements.text: that ... will generate ..., that ... might be redeployed ..., and that ... could eventually close ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 that절 보어

grammarElements.text: the lattice of startups clustered around Munich
grammarElements.label: 세 번째 that절의 주어

vocabulary.example: The tax cut is expected to produce downstream effects in consumer spending.

expressions.usage: 두 분야나 수준 사이의 차이를 줄이는 것을 설명할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The logic of [gamble] is that [effect 1], that [effect 2], and that [effect 3]
patterns.explanation: 위험한 전략의 근거를 삼중 that절로 체계적으로 나열하는 패턴
examples: The logic of the merger is that costs will fall, that talent will be retained, and that the combined firm will outpace rivals.
The logic of the stimulus is that spending will revive, that jobs will follow, and that tax revenue will eventually recover.

drillQuestions.question: The logic of Germany's gamble is that state-driven demand will generate ______ effects across the whole economy.

sentences.notes: 삼중 that절이 독일의 군사-경제 도박의 논리적 기둥 세 개를 명쾌하게 세우며, lattice라는 단어가 스타트업 생태계의 연결망을 시각화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The logic of Germany's gamble
generate downstream effects
the lattice of startups clustered around Munich
koreanAlignment.ko: 뮌헨 주변에 모여 있는 스타트업 네트워크

koreanAlignment.en: close the gap between military and civilian innovation
koreanAlignment.ko: 군사와 민간 혁신 사이의 격차를 좁히다

sentences.original: Yet for all its ambition, German recovery remains tantalisingly out of reach -- manufacturing output has fallen fifteen per cent from its 2018 peak, bottlenecks in facilities and labour supply have created huge order backlogs, and growth this year may prove even slower than the government's already-downgraded forecast of one per cent, prompting one bank analyst to compare the economy to a ketchup bottle that refuses to pour until, suddenly, everything comes out at once.
sentences.translation: 그러나 그 모든 야심에도 불구하고, 독일의 회복은 안타깝게도 여전히 손에 잡히지 않는다 -- 제조업 생산량은 2018년 정점 대비 15퍼센트 하락했고, 설비와 노동력 공급의 병목현상이 막대한 주문 적체를 만들어냈으며, 올해 성장률은 이미 하향 조정된 1퍼센트 전망보다도 더 느릴 수 있어, 한 은행 애널리스트가 이 경제를 쏟아지지 않다가 갑자기 한꺼번에 쏟아지는 케첩 병에 비유하기에 이르렀다.

grammarElements.text: Yet for all its ambition
grammarElements.label: 양보의 전치사구 (for all ~)

grammarElements.text: German recovery remains tantalisingly out of reach
grammarElements.label: 주절 (주어 + 동사 + 보어)

grammarElements.text: manufacturing output has fallen ..., bottlenecks have created ..., and growth may prove ...
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 삼중 병렬 설명

grammarElements.text: prompting one bank analyst to compare ...
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: A solution is tantalisingly close but not yet within grasp.

expressions.usage: '그렇게 대단한 계획에도 불구하고 결과는 미흡하다'는 뉘앙스를 전달하는 양보 표현

patterns.explanation: 노력이나 야심과 실제 결과 사이의 간극을 강조하는 양보 패턴
examples: Yet for all its promise, the technology remains frustratingly unreliable.
Yet for all its investment, the programme remains disappointingly behind schedule.

drillQuestions.question: German recovery remains tantalisingly out of ______.

sentences.notes: 케첩 병 비유가 독일 경제의 갑작스러운 회복 가능성과 현재의 정체를 기억에 남는 이미지로 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: for all its ambition
tantalisingly out of reach
bottlenecks in facilities and labour supply
a ketchup bottle that refuses to pour

sentences.original: Britain's economic data, meanwhile, present their own epistemological puzzle, since the Office for National Statistics relies on three imperfect data sources that it triangulates into an estimate so provisional that quarterly GDP growth typically does not stabilise for a decade, with revisions since 2012 increasing by roughly half owing to declining survey response rates -- a reminder that the numbers on which political narratives are built are shifting sands rather than solid ground.
sentences.translation: 한편, 영국의 경제 데이터는 그 자체로 인식론적 난제를 제시하는데, 국가통계청이 세 가지 불완전한 데이터 소스에 의존하여 삼각측정 방식으로 추정치를 산출하지만 그 추정치가 너무 잠정적이어서 분기별 GDP 성장률이 안정되기까지 통상 10년이 걸리며, 2012년 이후 설문 응답률 하락으로 수정폭이 약 50퍼센트 늘어났다 -- 정치적 서사가 세워지는 숫자가 단단한 땅이 아니라 움직이는 모래 위에 있다는 경고이다.

grammarElements.text: Britain's economic data
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: present their own epistemological puzzle
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: since the Office for National Statistics relies on ...
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (since)

grammarElements.text: shifting sands rather than solid ground
grammarElements.label: 은유적 보어

vocabulary.example: The debate raises epistemological questions about what we can actually know from the data.

expressions.phrase: shifting sands rather than solid ground
expressions.usage: 근거나 기반이 불안정하고 신뢰하기 어렵다는 것을 은유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Data/evidence] present their own [type of] puzzle, since [method] relies on [imperfect sources]
patterns.explanation: 데이터 자체의 신뢰성 문제를 제기하는 분석적 패턴
examples: Employment figures present their own methodological puzzle, since the survey relies on self-reported data that undercount gig workers.
Climate projections present their own statistical puzzle, since models rely on assumptions that diverge sharply after fifty years.

drillQuestions.question: Britain's economic data present their own ______ puzzle.

sentences.notes: shifting sands rather than solid ground라는 은유가 통계 자체의 불안정성을 짚으며, 정치적 서사의 근거 없음을 폭로한다.

koreanAlignment.en: epistemological puzzle
three imperfect data sources that it triangulates
koreanAlignment.ko: 세 가지 불완전한 데이터 소스를 삼각측정하여

koreanAlignment.en: so provisional that quarterly GDP growth does not stabilise for a decade
koreanAlignment.ko: 너무 잠정적이어서 분기별 GDP 성장률이 안정되기까지 10년이 걸리는

koreanAlignment.en: shifting sands rather than solid ground

sentences.original: The Iran crisis, which has sent gas prices spiking across the continent, has added a brutal layer of urgency to Europe's economic rethinking, presenting Britain's Treasury with the prospect of yet another expensive household bail-out while simultaneously making the case for energy-market integration with the EU -- an argument that even the most committed Brexiteers find difficult to rebut when petrol prices leap eight pence in a fortnight.
sentences.translation: 대륙 전역의 가스 가격을 급등시킨 이란 위기는 유럽의 경제적 재고에 가혹한 긴급성의 층을 더했으며, 영국 재무부에 또 하나의 값비싼 가계 긴급 지원의 가능성을 제시하는 동시에 EU와의 에너지 시장 통합을 옹호하는 근거를 만들어냈다 -- 주유 가격이 2주 만에 8펜스 뛰면 가장 확고한 브렉시트 지지자도 반박하기 어려운 논거이다.

grammarElements.text: The Iran crisis, which has sent gas prices spiking
grammarElements.label: 관계절이 삽입된 주어

grammarElements.text: has added
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: a brutal layer of urgency
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: presenting ... while simultaneously making the case for ...
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (동시 행위)

vocabulary.example: The minister struggled to rebut the opposition's claims during the debate.

expressions.usage: 논거를 세우며 특정 방안을 정당화할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Crisis] has added [urgency] to [rethinking], presenting [entity] with [challenge] while simultaneously making the case for [solution]
patterns.explanation: 위기가 기존 논의에 긴급성을 더하면서 동시에 해법을 부각시키는 패턴
examples: The cyberattack has added urgency to the security review, presenting the board with new costs while simultaneously making the case for outsourced IT defence.
The flood has added urgency to the climate debate, presenting local councils with repair bills while simultaneously making the case for green infrastructure.

drillQuestions.question: The Iran crisis has added a brutal layer of ______ to Europe's economic rethinking.

sentences.notes: presenting과 making the case for가 동시에 작동하며, 위기가 실용적 해법(EU 에너지 통합)의 논거가 되는 역설적 상황을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: sent gas prices spiking
a brutal layer of urgency
making the case for energy-market integration with the EU
koreanAlignment.ko: EU와의 에너지 시장 통합을 옹호하는 근거

koreanAlignment.en: petrol prices leap eight pence in a fortnight

sentences.original: Paradoxically, motoring in Britain has rarely been cheaper in real terms, because fuel taxes are so high that pump prices are less responsive to global oil-market gyrations, because fleet-wide fuel efficiency has improved by two-thirds over three decades, and because fourteen years of frozen fuel duty -- costing taxpayers a hundred and twenty billion pounds -- have insulated drivers from the true cost of their consumption.
sentences.translation: 역설적으로, 영국에서의 자동차 운전은 실질 기준으로 이보다 더 저렴했던 적이 거의 없다. 연료세가 너무 높아 주유소 가격이 국제 유가 변동에 덜 민감하기 때문이고, 차량 전체의 연비가 30년간 3분의 2나 향상되었기 때문이며, 납세자에게 1200억 파운드의 비용을 초래한 14년간의 동결된 연료세가 운전자들을 실제 소비 비용으로부터 차단해 왔기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: Paradoxically
grammarElements.label: 부사 (문두 전환)

grammarElements.text: motoring in Britain has rarely been cheaper in real terms
grammarElements.label: 주절 (주어 + 동사 + 보어)

grammarElements.text: because ..., because ..., and because ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 because절

vocabulary.example: Markets have grown accustomed to the gyrations of oil prices.

expressions.usage: 명목 가격이 아닌 물가 조정 후 실질 가치를 비교할 때 사용하는 경제 용어

patterns.pattern: Paradoxically, [subject] has rarely been [more/less adjective], because [reason 1], because [reason 2], and because [reason 3]
patterns.explanation: 직관에 반하는 사실을 제시하고 삼중 이유로 설명하는 패턴
examples: Paradoxically, food has rarely been safer, because regulation has tightened, because testing has improved, and because supply chains have become more transparent.
Paradoxically, air travel has rarely been cheaper, because competition has intensified, because fuel efficiency has risen, and because ancillary revenues have subsidised base fares.

drillQuestions.question: Pump prices are less responsive to global oil-market ______.

sentences.notes: 삼중 because절로 직관에 반하는 결론(운전이 저렴해짐)을 논리적으로 뒷받침하며, 14년간 동결된 연료세의 숨은 비용을 폭로한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Paradoxically, motoring in Britain has rarely been cheaper in real terms
koreanAlignment.ko: 역설적으로, 영국에서의 자동차 운전은 실질 기준으로 이보다 더 저렴했던 적이 거의 없다

koreanAlignment.en: global oil-market gyrations
fleet-wide fuel efficiency
insulated drivers from the true cost

sentences.original: What unites these disparate threads -- Britain's tilt back toward Brussels, Germany's attempt to rearm its way to growth, the unreliability of GDP statistics, and the hidden cheapness of British motoring -- is a continent struggling to reconcile inherited economic models with a world in which America is turning inward, China is dumping surplus production, and the old certainties of free trade and transatlantic alliance have been replaced by a scramble for strategic autonomy.
sentences.translation: 영국의 브뤼셀 쪽으로의 회귀, 독일의 군비 확장을 통한 성장 시도, GDP 통계의 신뢰 불가능성, 영국 자동차 운전의 숨겨진 저렴함이라는 서로 다른 갈래를 하나로 묶는 것은, 미국이 내향으로 돌아서고 중국이 과잉 생산물을 덤핑하며 자유무역과 대서양 동맹의 오랜 확실성이 전략적 자율성을 향한 쟁탈전으로 대체된 세계에서 물려받은 경제 모델과 화해하려 분투하는 대륙이다.

grammarElements.text: What unites these disparate threads
grammarElements.label: What 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: be동사

grammarElements.text: a continent struggling to reconcile ...
grammarElements.label: 보어 (현재분사 수식)

grammarElements.text: in which America is turning inward, China is dumping ..., and the old certainties ... have been replaced by ...
grammarElements.label: 관계절 내 삼중 병렬

vocabulary.example: The report connects several disparate data points into a coherent narrative.

expressions.usage: 여러 주체가 제한된 자원이나 지위를 두고 경쟁하는 상황을 묘사

patterns.pattern: What unites these disparate [elements] is [overarching theme]
patterns.explanation: 여러 소재를 하나의 상위 주제로 통합하는 종합 패턴
examples: What unites these disparate crises is a system that rewards short-term thinking.
What unites these disparate reforms is a government desperate to show results before the next election.

drillQuestions.question: What unites these ______ threads is a continent struggling to reconcile inherited economic models.

sentences.notes: What unites 구문이 글의 다양한 소재를 하나의 거시적 서사(유럽의 경제적 정체성 모색)로 묶어내는 종합 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: these disparate threads
struggling to reconcile inherited economic models
America is turning inward
a scramble for strategic autonomy

pronunciationNotes: disparate: /ˈdɪs.pər.ət/ 첫 음절에 강세, 마지막은 약화된 /ət/

sentences.original: Europe's emerging economic identity, if it has one, is defined not by the bold confidence of a superpower but by the pragmatic recognition that alignment with neighbours is preferable to isolation, that fiscal orthodoxy must sometimes yield to strategic necessity, and that the most honest measure of economic health is not the headline GDP figure but whether the institutions and infrastructure exist to deliver prosperity that citizens can actually feel -- a test that neither Britain nor Germany, for all their statistical revisions and fiscal ambitions, has yet convincingly passed.
sentences.translation: 유럽의 떠오르는 경제적 정체성은 -- 그런 것이 있다면 -- 초강대국의 대담한 자신감이 아니라, 이웃과의 협력이 고립보다 낫다는 실용적 인식, 재정 정통주의가 때로 전략적 필요에 양보해야 한다는 인식, 그리고 경제 건강의 가장 정직한 척도는 헤드라인 GDP 수치가 아니라 시민이 실제로 느낄 수 있는 번영을 전달할 제도와 인프라가 존재하는지 여부라는 인식에 의해 정의된다 -- 영국도 독일도, 그 모든 통계 수정과 재정적 야심에도 불구하고, 아직 설득력 있게 통과하지 못한 시험이다.

grammarElements.text: Europe's emerging economic identity, if it has one
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 삽입 조건절

grammarElements.text: is defined not by ... but by ...
grammarElements.label: not A but B 수동태

grammarElements.text: that alignment ... is preferable ..., that fiscal orthodoxy must yield ..., and that the most honest measure ... is not ... but whether ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 that절

grammarElements.text: a test that neither Britain nor Germany has yet convincingly passed
grammarElements.label: 동격 + neither A nor B

vocabulary.example: The compromise reflected a pragmatic rather than ideological approach.
Fiscal orthodoxy must sometimes yield to strategic necessity.

expressions.usage: 노력이나 규모에도 불구하고 결과가 미흡할 때 쓰는 양보 표현

patterns.pattern: [Identity] is defined not by [A] but by the pragmatic recognition that [principle 1], that [principle 2], and that [principle 3]
patterns.explanation: 정체성이나 본질을 부정과 긍정의 대비로 정의하고, 원칙을 삼중으로 나열하는 결론 패턴
examples: The company's culture is defined not by its slogans but by the recognition that talent needs autonomy, that failure must be tolerated, and that the best ideas rarely come from the top.
The alliance's value is defined not by its military might but by the recognition that cooperation reduces risk, that shared intelligence multiplies strength, and that no member can prosper alone.

drillQuestions.question: Europe's emerging economic identity is defined not by the bold confidence of a superpower but by the ______ recognition.

sentences.notes: if it has one이라는 삽입절이 유럽 정체성의 불확실성을 한마디로 집약하며, 마지막 대시 뒤의 결론이 글 전체의 핵심 판정을 내린다.

koreanAlignment.en: Europe's emerging economic identity, if it has one
koreanAlignment.ko: 유럽의 떠오르는 경제적 정체성은, 그런 것이 있다면

koreanAlignment.en: defined not by the bold confidence of a superpower
fiscal orthodoxy must sometimes yield to strategic necessity
koreanAlignment.ko: 재정 정통주의가 때로 전략적 필요에 양보해야 한다

koreanAlignment.en: a test that neither Britain nor Germany has yet convincingly passed
koreanAlignment.ko: 영국도 독일도 아직 설득력 있게 통과하지 못한 시험
